---
title: "UIAO_103 Spec-Test Coverage"
description: "Auto-generated invariant inventory + manual test-coverage map."
uiao.id: governance-spec-test-coverage
uiao.status: Current
provenance:
  derived_from:
    - src/uiao/canon/specs/*.md
    - src/uiao/canon/UIAO_*.md
  derived_by: scripts/tools/spec_test_audit.py
---

# UIAO_103 Spec-Test Coverage

This page tracks two artifacts:

1. **Invariant inventory** — every RFC 2119 normative keyword (MUST,
   SHALL, REQUIRED, MUST NOT, SHALL NOT, RECOMMENDED, SHOULD) found in
   canonical spec markdown. Auto-generated by
   `scripts/tools/spec_test_audit.py`. Re-run on every PR; the
   coverage gate enforces that **per-spec invariant counts never
   shrink**.

2. **Coverage map** — manually maintained mapping from spec
   `document_id` to the test files (or pytest nodeids) that exercise
   its invariants. Authors update this when wiring new tests.

## Invariant inventory (auto-generated)

<!-- BEGIN AUTO -->
_This table is auto-generated by `scripts/tools/spec_test_audit.py`._

| document_id | file | total | by_kind |
|---|---|---:|---|
| UIAO_121 | `src/uiao/canon/specs/adapter-conformance-test-plan-template.md` | 1 | MUST=1 |
| UIAO_122 | `src/uiao/canon/specs/adapter-developer-training-program.md` | 1 | MUST=1 |
| UIAO_123 | `src/uiao/canon/specs/adapter-integration-test-plan.md` | 1 | MUST=1 |

**Total invariants tracked:** 3
**Specs with at least one invariant:** 3
<!-- END AUTO -->

## Coverage map (manual)

```yaml
# document_id → tests that exercise its invariants. Pytest nodeids
# accepted: tests/test_foo.py or tests/test_foo.py::test_specific_invariant.
# The CI gate does not validate these paths — author discipline is the
# enforcement mechanism. Keep entries sorted by document_id.

UIAO_100:
  - tests/test_orchestrator_scheduler.py
  - tests/test_e2e.py::TestOrchestratorSchedulerE2E
  - tests/test_terraform_scheduler_integration.py

UIAO_113:
  - tests/test_evidence_graph.py
  - tests/test_evidence_graph_scheduler.py
  - tests/test_evidence_graph_drift_semantic.py

UIAO_016:
  # DRIFT-SEMANTIC freshness evaluator
  - tests/test_drift_semantic_freshness.py
```

## Maintenance

- **Add a normative invariant to a spec.** No action required. The audit
  picks it up on the next PR. The coverage gate accepts the count
  growing.
- **Lose a normative invariant.** Either the gate fires (count shrinks
  → PR fails) or the loss is intentional (spec retired); update the
  committed table with `python scripts/tools/spec_test_coverage_check.py
  --update` and explain in the PR description.
- **Wire a test that exercises a spec.** Add the test path under the
  appropriate `document_id` in the coverage map. The map is
  documentation; no automation reads it.

## CI

The `spec-test-coverage.yml` workflow runs on every PR touching canon
specs or this file. Failure modes:

- Spec's invariant count dropped → block.
- Spec previously tracked is missing entirely → block.
- New invariant added → pass (with a notice listing the new lines).
